import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import time
import gc

# Import our implementation
exec(open('abmil_assignment.py').read())

def performance_and_gradient_tests():
    print('=== Performance and Gradient Tests ===')
    
    # Test 5: Large input test (memory efficiency)
    print('\n5. Large input performance test...')
    model = ABMIL(input_dim=320, hidden_dim=64)
    model.eval()
    
    # Test with 10K patches
    print('  Testing with 10,000 patches...')
    start_time = time.time()
    X_large = torch.randn(10000, 320, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits, attention = model(X_large)
    
    end_time = time.time()
    
    assert logits.shape == (1, 2), f'Expected logits shape (1, 2), got {logits.shape}'
    assert attention.shape == (10000, 1), f'Expected attention shape (10000, 1), got {attention.shape}'
    assert torch.allclose(attention.sum(), torch.tensor(1.0), atol=1e-5), 'Attention weights should sum to 1'
    
    elapsed_time = end_time - start_time
    print(f'  Time for 10K patches: {elapsed_time:.3f} seconds')
    assert elapsed_time < 30, f'Processing should be < 30s, took {elapsed_time:.3f}s'
    
    # Clean up memory
    del X_large, logits, attention
    gc.collect()
    
    print('✓ Large input performance passed')
    
    # Test 6: Gradient flow
    print('\n6. Gradient flow test...')
    model.train()
    torch.manual_seed(123)
    X = torch.randn(50, 320, requires_grad=True)
    
    logits, attention = model(X)
    loss = logits.sum()
    loss.backward()
    
    # Check that gradients exist and are finite
    assert X.grad is not None, 'Input should have gradients'
    assert torch.isfinite(X.grad).all(), 'Input gradients should be finite'
    
    # Check model parameter gradients
    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
        assert param.grad is not None, f'Parameter {name} should have gradients'
        if param.dtype in [torch.float32, torch.float64]:  # Skip gradient check for float16
            assert torch.isfinite(param.grad).all(), f'Parameter {name} gradients should be finite'
    
    print('✓ Gradient flow passed')
    
    # Test 7: Numerical stability with different input scales
    print('\n7. Numerical stability test...')
    model.eval()
    
    # Test with very small values
    X_small = torch.randn(50, 320) * 1e-6
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits_small, attention_small = model(X_small)
    assert torch.isfinite(logits_small).all(), 'Logits should be finite with small inputs'
    assert torch.isfinite(attention_small).all(), 'Attention should be finite with small inputs'
    
    # Test with large values 
    X_large = torch.randn(50, 320) * 1e3
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits_large, attention_large = model(X_large)
    assert torch.isfinite(logits_large).all(), 'Logits should be finite with large inputs'
    assert torch.isfinite(attention_large).all(), 'Attention should be finite with large inputs'
    
    print('✓ Numerical stability passed')
    
    print('\n=== All performance and gradient tests passed! ===')
    return True

# Run the test
result = performance_and_gradient_tests()
